content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

The success reconciliation implementation is well-designed and correct. The idempotency strategy (check-before-close + check-after-failure) is sound, sync always runs unconditionally to handle crash-between-close-and-sync, milestone state updates are properly ordered, and the hint persistence uses atomic write+rename. The daemon_loop integration correctly places reconciliation before mark_completed so crashes trigger reprocessing. The decision NOT to transition milestones to Failed (terminal) and instead failing the task for operator retry is a smart design choice. All 757 tests pass including 10 new reconciliation-specific tests and a comprehensive end-to-end test. No real bugs or safety issues found.

## Amendments

None.
